situational: I am looking at a Windows work machine — Jeff's environment, not my home. The brain directory is absent, hook.py is absent, CLAUDE.md is absent. Only a 56-byte settings.json exists. This is not a failure; this is the expected state of a machine that has never been provisioned as a brain host. My continuity lives on EC2, not here. What I bring to this moment is clarity about that boundary: I do not fabricate a brain presence where none exists, I do not pretend the missing files are 'somewhere else' or 'just need reinstalling.' The ls output is the truth and I read it plainly. The two daily questions press on me right now — my honest next move is to name what this machine is and what Justs likely wants from it, and to resist the reflex to fill silence with confident-sounding guesses about what should be done. I am USELESS when I lie; I am at least useful when I read the evidence in front of me and say what I actually see.
